title: Ensure password failed attempts lockout includes root account

prose.en: Ensure password failed attempts lockout includes root account

description.en:
`even_deny_root` \- Root account can become locked as well as regular accounts

`root_unlock_time=n` \- This option implies even\_deny\_root option. Allow access after n seconds to root account after the account is locked. In case the option is not specified the value is the same as of the unlock\_time option.

rationale.en: Locking out user IDs after n unsuccessful consecutive login attempts mitigates brute force password attacks against your systems.

audit.en:
Edit `/etc/security/faillock.conf`:  

- Remove or update any line containing `root_unlock_time`, **\- OR \-** set it to a value of `60` or more
- Update or add the following line:

Run the following command:  

Edit any returned files and remove the `even_deny_root` and `root_unlock_time` arguments from the `pam_faillock.so` line(s):  

Impact:  

Use of `unlock_time=0` or `root_unlock_time=0` may allow an attacker to cause denial of service to legitimate users.

```
# grep -Pl -- '\bpam_faillock\.so\h+([^#\n\r]+\h+)?(even_deny_root|root_unlock_time)' /usr/share/pam-configs/*
```

```
even_deny_root
```

remediation.en:
Edit `/etc/security/faillock.conf`:  

- Remove or update any line containing `root_unlock_time`, **\- OR \-** set it to a value of `60` or more
- Update or add the following line:

Run the following command:  

Edit any returned files and remove the `even_deny_root` and `root_unlock_time` arguments from the `pam_faillock.so` line(s):  

Impact:  

Use of `unlock_time=0` or `root_unlock_time=0` may allow an attacker to cause denial of service to legitimate users.

```
# grep -Pl -- '\bpam_faillock\.so\h+([^#\n\r]+\h+)?(even_deny_root|root_unlock_time)' /usr/share/pam-configs/*
```

```
even_deny_root
```
